reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Code reading verified. At eip7594.c:877, compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge receives commitments_bytes (original array with duplicates) instead of unique_commitments (deduplicated array). The function hashes the first num_commitments elements from the ORIGINAL array (lines 423-427), where num_commitments has been updated to the deduplicated count. This violates the specification (SG-006) which requires hashing the deduplicated commitments. Different orderings of semantically equivalent inputs produce different challenge values, breaking the soundness of the Fiat-Shamir batch verification. Per 01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json line 42-43: 'Commitment deduplication must preserve semantic equivalence — mapping indices must be bijective per unique commitment.' The code correctly deduplicates but incorrectly uses the original array for challenge computation. Severity calibrated against BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json: c-kzg-4844 has <5% network share (TARGET_INFO.json shows ethereum/c-kzg-4844 is a library used by multiple clients, but affects only c-kzg-4844 users, estimated <33% but >5% network), capped at Medium per severity_classification thresholds.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: 01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json, property PROP-57888860-inv-003: 'Commitment deduplication must preserve semantic equivalence — mapping indices must be bijective per unique commitment.' Specification SG-021 (verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch) and SG-006 (compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge) require hashing deduplicated commitments in canonical order.
